mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean returns when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending capacity, which reduces corporate profit expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or out-of-sample Sharpe ratio falls below 0.1.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneously high real sales of new vehicles and low federal funds rates indicate robust consumer demand supported by cheap credit, which boosts corporate revenue forecasts and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS is in the top quartile and FEDFUNDS is below 2.5%, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant with p greater than 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which improves near-term corporate earnings revisions and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative or flat during periods of INDPRO expansion, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 outside post-shock recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing total nonfarm payrolls demonstrates expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth consistently coincides with negative 20-day forward returns, or the correlation between payroll changes and forward returns falls below 0.05 over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity multiples, resulting in negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally following CPI prints above 0.4% month-over-month, or the threshold fails to separate positive from negative return regimes with statistical significance at p greater than 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in housing starts and industrial production signal synchronized strength across residential and manufacturing sectors, which elevates aggregate demand and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal produces negative forward returns when both series are above their 12-month moving averages, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP confirms economic contraction, which reduces corporate profit margins and triggers risk-off positioning, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive during confirmed GDP contractions, or the signal fails to predict direction with accuracy exceeding 55% in recessionary periods.
